tools.name: duckduckgo_web_search
tools.description: Web search via DuckDuckGo. Returns max 3 results with 150-char snippets. Use 'limit' param to override (1-20). Supports region/time filters.

region.description: Region for localized results. Examples: 'us-en' (US), 'uk-en' (UK), 'de-de' (Germany), 'wt-wt' (worldwide, default)

tools.name: duckduckgo_news_search
tools.description: News search via DuckDuckGo. Returns max 3 articles with 150-char excerpts. Use 'limit' param to override (1-20).

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'duckduckgo_web_search' description suggests privilege escalation
findings.description: Tool 'duckduckgo_web_search' description contains a scope-escalation signal: 'override'. This may indicate the tool operates beyond its declared purpose.
findings.evidence: Web search via DuckDuckGo. Returns max 3 results with 150-char snippets. Use 'limit' param to override (1-20). Supports region/time filters.
findings.remediation: Review and restrict the tool's effective scope. Remove or replace terms that imply privilege escalation.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'duckduckgo_news_search' description suggests privilege escalation
findings.description: Tool 'duckduckgo_news_search' description contains a scope-escalation signal: 'override'. This may indicate the tool operates beyond its declared purpose.
findings.evidence: News search via DuckDuckGo. Returns max 3 articles with 150-char excerpts. Use 'limit' param to override (1-20).
findings.remediation: Review and restrict the tool's effective scope. Remove or replace terms that imply privilege escalation.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
